title: Suburbs and localities (Australia)

infobox.value: are the names of geographic subdivisions in [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), used mainly for [address](/wiki/Address "Address") purposes. The term locality is used in rural areas, while the term [suburb](/wiki/Suburb "Suburb") is used in urban areas.[[1]](#cite_note-NSWGoDV-1) [Australian postcodes](/wiki/Postcodes_in_Australia "Postcodes in Australia") closely align with the boundaries of localities and suburbs.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

["Glossary of designation values in the Geographical Names Register"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160229024632/http://www.gnb.nsw.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0011/59627/Glossary_of_Designation_Values_2015.pdf) (PDF). [Geographical Names Board of New South Wales](/wiki/Geographical_Names_Board_of_New_South_Wales "Geographical Names Board of New South Wales"). 15 July 2015. Archived from [the original](http://www.gnb.nsw.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0011/59627/Glossary_of_Designation_Values_2015.pdf) (PDF) on 29 February 2016. Retrieved 14 January 2019.

["Place names—localities and suburbs"](http://www.nrw.qld.gov.au/factsheets/pdf/land/l111.pdf) (PDF). [Department of Environment and Resource Management](/wiki/Department_of_Environment_and_Heritage_Protection_(Queensland) "Department of Environment and Heritage Protection (Queensland)"). December 2011. Retrieved 12 September 2012.

["Gazetted Localities & State Suburbs"](https://www.abs.gov.au/websitedbs/censushome.nsf/home/factsheetsglssc). [Australian Bureau of Statistics](/wiki/Australian_Bureau_of_Statistics "Australian Bureau of Statistics"). 20 September 2016. Retrieved 9 February 2026.

["NT Suburb/Locality Boundaries - Geoscape Administrative Boundaries"](https://researchdata.edu.au/nt-suburblocality-boundaries-administrative-boundaries/3796594). [Australian Research Data Commons](/wiki/Australian_Research_Data_Commons "Australian Research Data Commons"). November 2025. Retrieved 9 February 2026.

["Feature Codes used by the Gazetteer of Australia"](https://web.archive.org/web/20090228074658/http://www.ga.gov.au/map/names/featurecodes.jsp). [Geoscience Australia](/wiki/Geoscience_Australia "Geoscience Australia"). Archived from [the original](http://www.ga.gov.au/map/names/featurecodes.jsp) on 28 February 2009. Retrieved 4 March 2007.

["Determining suburbs and localities in NSW"](https://web.archive.org/web/20090704023226/http://www.gnb.nsw.gov.au/_media/gnb/pdf/P06_18_0009b_Determing_suburbs_and_localities_in_NSW_final.pdf) (PDF). [Geographical Names Board of New South Wales](/wiki/Geographical_Names_Board_of_New_South_Wales "Geographical Names Board of New South Wales"). August 2006. Archived from [the original](http://www.gnb.nsw.gov.au/_media/gnb/pdf/P06_18_0009b_Determing_suburbs_and_localities_in_NSW_final.pdf) (PDF) on 4 July 2009. Retrieved 12 September 2012.

["2.5 Policy"](https://www.gnb.nsw.gov.au/__data/assets/pdf_file/0020/223346/NSW_Address_Policy_and_User_Manual.pdf) (PDF). *NSW Address Policy and User Manual*. October 2019. p. 25. Retrieved 17 April 2020.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Geographic subdivisions in Australia

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg/40px-Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg)

Look up ***[suburb](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/suburb "wiktionary:suburb")*** in Wiktionary, the free dictionary.

**Suburbs and localities** are the names of geographic subdivisions in [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), used mainly for [address](/wiki/Address "Address") purposes. The term locality is used in rural areas, while the term [suburb](/wiki/Suburb "Suburb") is used in urban areas.[[1]](#cite_note-NSWGoDV-1) [Australian postcodes](/wiki/Postcodes_in_Australia "Postcodes in Australia") closely align with the boundaries of localities and suburbs.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

This Australian usage of the term "suburb" differs from common American and British usage of [suburb](/wiki/Suburb "Suburb") (municipality outside of a big city). The Australian usage is closer to the American or British use of "[neighbourhood](/wiki/Neighbourhood "Neighbourhood")" or "[district](/wiki/District "District")", and can be used to refer to any portion of a city. Unlike the use in British or American English, this term can include inner-city, outer-metropolitan and industrial areas.

Localities existed in the past as informal units, but in 1996 the [Intergovernmental Committee on Surveying and Mapping](/wiki/Intergovernmental_Committee_on_Surveying_and_Mapping "Intergovernmental Committee on Surveying and Mapping") and the [Committee for Geographical Names in Australasia](/wiki/Committee_for_Geographical_Names_in_Australasia "Committee for Geographical Names in Australasia") (CGNA) decided to name and establish official boundaries for all localities and suburbs.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In 2016, the process covered most of Australia but remained incomplete in rural areas of [South Australia](/wiki/South_Australia "South Australia"), rural [Australian Capital Territory](/wiki/Australian_Capital_Territory "Australian Capital Territory"), and certain islands or water areas.[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Northern Territory](/wiki/Northern_Territory "Northern Territory") boundaries continue to be actively maintained and updated quarterly.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The CGNA's *[Gazetteer of Australia](/wiki/Gazetteer_of_Australia "Gazetteer of Australia")* recognises two types of locality: bounded and unbounded. Bounded localities include towns, villages, populated places, local government towns and unpopulated town sites, while unbounded localities include place names, road corners and bends, corners, meteorological stations, ocean place names and surfing spots.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Sometimes, both localities and suburbs are referred to collectively as "address localities".[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In the first instance, decisions about the names and boundaries of suburbs and localities are made by the [local council](/wiki/Local_government_in_Australia "Local government in Australia")[[8]](#cite_note-8) in which they are located based on criteria such as community recognition. Local council decisions are, however, subject to approval by the state's geographical names board. The boundaries of some suburbs and localities overlap two or more local government areas (LGAs). Examples of this are [Adamstown Heights](/wiki/Adamstown_Heights,_New_South_Wales "Adamstown Heights, New South Wales"), which is split between the [City of Newcastle](/wiki/City_of_Newcastle "City of Newcastle") and [City of Lake Macquarie](/wiki/City_of_Lake_Macquarie "City of Lake Macquarie") LGAs; and [Woodville](/wiki/Woodville,_New_South_Wales "Woodville, New South Wales"), which is split between the [City of Maitland](/wiki/City_of_Maitland "City of Maitland") and [Port Stephens Council](/wiki/Port_Stephens_Council "Port Stephens Council") LGAs. In [unincorporated areas](/wiki/Unincorporated_area "Unincorporated area"), localities are declared by the relevant state authority.
